candidate_source_families_consulted.name: model-registry.ts subject under analysis
candidate_source_families_consulted.extracted: 858-LOC TS file. Top-half (1-228) is typebox schema graph with Compile() at line 180. Class ModelRegistry (301-831) has 17 public/private methods. Two deep-merge helpers (mergeCompat 234-263, applyModelOverride 269-293). Dynamic provider registration block 718-830 calls registerOAuthProvider + registerApiProvider from @need-singularity/hive-ai/oauth and @need-singularity/hive-ai. Schema validation produces TLocalizedValidationError → formatValidationPath at 184-195. ProviderConfigInput export interface 836-858 defines the extension surface.

candidate_source_families_consulted.name: internal callsite inventory
candidate_source_families_consulted.extracted: 10 distinct external consumers within coding-agent. Any port that breaks ModelRegistry's public surface (create/inMemory/refresh/getAll/getAvailable/find/hasConfiguredAuth/getApiKeyAndHeaders/getApiKeyForProvider/isUsingOAuth/registerProvider/unregisterProvider) cascades to all 10. This is the primary cost gate.

candidate_source_families_consulted.name: kick architecture witness (cross-cycle precedent)
candidate_source_families_consulted.extracted: axis_backend_adapter mandates 6-method Adapter trait (prepare_prompt / spawn / stream / capture_witness / teardown / cost_estimate) per backend; axis_registry mandates state/kick/registry/backends/<name>.hexa as on-disk registry. Architecture says backend metadata lives in .hexa; nothing in the architecture says model-registry.ts MUST be ported — registry is for *kick backends* (claude_code/codex_cli/gemini_cli CLIs as ω-cycle executors), NOT for the runtime model registry of the coding-agent process itself. This is a critical scope boundary that defuses the apparent pressure to port.

candidate_source_families_consulted.name: existing kick backend .hexa shape
candidate_source_families_consulted.extracted: Indented key/value DSL, 29 lines, declares slug + cli-binary + invocation + 6 hook function names + sentinel/err formats + slot-pool / slot-policy / multi-slot CLI evidence. This is the *target shape* a hypothetical port would land in. By inspection: this file is METADATA, not LOGIC. The 858 LOC TS subject is mostly LOGIC (typebox schemas + deep merges + auth resolution + dynamic registration). Shape impedance mismatch is the second cost gate.

candidate_source_families_consulted.name: adjacent dependency surface
candidate_source_families_consulted.extracted: model-registry.ts imports from auth-storage.ts (AuthStorage class — OAuth credential lifecycle, hasAuth, getApiKey, getOAuthProviders) and resolve-config-value.ts (env var + command substitution for apiKey/headers). Both are TS-runtime-bound. Total transitive surface that would need to move with any non-trivial port: 858 + 493 + 142 = 1493 LOC.

axes_surfaced.name: port target & strategy
options_considered: A. Full port to nexus/state/<...>/registry .hexa DSL (REJECTED — see verdict below)
B. Metadata-only shim: codegen TS-source-of-truth → .hexa generated artifact (CHOSEN as conditional Tier-1 if downstream nexus tooling has demonstrated need; otherwise NO-OP control wins)
C. No-op control: keep model-registry.ts as-is, no port (CHOSEN as default Tier-1 — F2 enumerates this as a real candidate, not a strawman)
D. Port to standalone npm package within hive monorepo (REJECTED — same TS runtime, only changes packaging; ROI ≈ 0 for the stated kick topic)
E. Port to Rust for compile-time schema validation + redistribution (REJECTED — typebox→serde rewrite ≈ 2-3 weeks, breaks 10 callsites, no concrete consumer demand witnessed)
axes_surfaced.decision: B-conditional + C-default. NO full port. The architecture witness's axis_registry already places kick-backend metadata in .hexa — that scope does NOT extend to the coding-agent runtime model registry. If a future kick demonstrates a non-TS nexus consumer needing model metadata (e.g., nexus kick bench needing per-model cost-per-token to score backends), THEN emit a generated .hexa shim from TS source-of-truth (one-directional codegen, never hand-edited). Until that consumer exists, C wins.
axes_surfaced.rationale: α-noise primed reframe: 'SoT split = adversary' rules out any A/D/E that creates two source-of-truth surfaces (models.json + getModels in TS, plus a hand-edited .hexa duplicate). γ-noise icecube reframe: thin metadata shim over dense logic volume is the only port shape that doesn't require rewriting the auth/OAuth/typebox compute. β-noise expanded the choice set from 2 (port/don't) to 5 (A..E), exposing D and E as red herrings that look like 'port' but don't address the kick topic.

None of the five is currently witnessed (all are hypothetical future triggers). Therefore: C-default holds *until* one of these reasons appears in a future drill round.
axes_surfaced.rationale: Falsifier coverage at the proposal level (not just the witness level): the chosen strategy is auto-falsifiable by any of 5 named conditions, each with a pre-prescribed corrective action. This is the drill-stratum equivalent of raw 71 falsifier-retire — proposal is retired by witnessed condition, not by argument.

axes_surfaced.decision: C dominates A/D/E on every column. B dominates A/D/E on every column except sot_split_risk where it ties C. Therefore C is the no-regret default and B is the upgrade path.
axes_surfaced.rationale: F1 satisfied: 6 numeric/categorical columns × 5 options = 30 scored cells. Decision is mechanical from the table, not argued.

axes_surfaced.name: audit-time inside-vs-outside-the-well distinguishability of this cycle's creative leaps

creative_leaps_in_this_cycle.inside_well_alternative_rejected: 'just brainstorm port shapes harder' — would have produced LLM-trained-prior recombination of standard refactor patterns; the icecube neutrino topology is NOT in standard refactor literature, distinguishability is mechanical
'port or don't port' — the binary framing is the LLM-default; randomized expansion to 5 is what surfaced D/E as red herrings
'split is fine, just keep them in sync' — would have downplayed the cost; the absorbed jaccard primitive is a witnessed prior measurement of exactly this drift dynamic

axes_surfaced.decision: Each creative leap in this cycle's reasoning is source-tagged to a hash-anchored outside-the-well envelope. At audit, a reviewer can trace any non-default conclusion back to the byte source that displaced the LLM training prior.
axes_surfaced.rationale: F5 + F11 + F13 (architecture-witness falsifiers inherited): no creative move in this witness is sourced from inside-the-well recombination alone. Even the choice of which 4 axes to fan out (axis_design_strategy + axis_falsifier_coverage from kick input + axis_perf_comparability + axis_noise_distinguishability added by this cycle) is justified by the K≥3 raw 48 mandate, not by free generation.

tier_1_promotions.rationale: Option C (no port) dominates A/D/E on all 6 quantified columns and ties B on the only column where C does not strictly dominate (sot_split_risk both 'none' / 'low'). 0 LOC rewritten, 0 callsites updated, 0 engineer-days, 0 SoT split risk. The 858 LOC TS subject + 635 LOC adjacent dependencies + 10 internal callsites are not load-bearing for any witnessed nexus consumer today. Architecture witness's axis_registry .hexa scope is for kick-backend executor metadata (claude_code/codex_cli/gemini_cli CLIs), NOT for the runtime model registry of the coding-agent process — this scope distinction defuses the apparent pressure to port.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: passes F1 (loc=0, days=0, risk=none — quantified), F2 (5 options enumerated), F6 (no deletion proposed — preserves model-registry.ts entirely), F10 (this entry cites its falsifier IDs)

tier_1_promotions.rationale: Latent (not active) Tier-1: if and when a non-TS nexus consumer demonstrates need for model metadata (e.g., nexus kick bench scoring backends by per-model cost-per-token), promote B from latent to active. Implementation = single codegen tool (~50 LOC) reading TS source-of-truth (models.json + getModels output) and emitting .hexa with GENERATED-DO-NOT-EDIT header. One-directional only — .hexa is a derived artifact, never hand-edited, eliminating SoT split. 0.5-1 engineer-days when triggered. Trigger condition (raw 66): reason=non-ts-consumer-demonstrated, fix=emit tool/model_registry_hexa_codegen.hexa with codegen-only contract.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: passes F1 (0.5-1 days, ~50 LOC, low risk — quantified), F6 (TS retained as SoT), F9 (latent state allows future drill rounds to promote without re-architecting), F10 (cites its falsifier IDs), F12 (trigger uses reason+fix)

tier_1_promotions.rationale: C-default is conditionally retained: 5 named failure modes (axis_falsifier_coverage.failure_modes_inventory), each in raw 66 reason+fix shape, would each individually invalidate C and trigger a specific corrective. None of the 5 is currently witnessed. This Tier-1 IS the proposal-level falsifier contract — it makes C retirable by mechanical condition rather than by re-argument. Concrete units: 5 named conditions × 1 prescribed fix each = 5 retirement levers.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: passes F12 (every failure mode has reason+fix), F9 (drill-stratum retirement contract), F10 (cites IDs), F1 (5 named retirement triggers is the quantified unit)

obstructions.step_1_identification: no current nexus tooling consumes model-registry metadata; B-conditional cannot be promoted to active without a witnessed consumer
obstructions.step_2_documentation: OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED: ROI is conditional on a future event. Drill-round 1 cannot finalize B as active.
obstructions.step_3_structural_reframe: Recast B as LATENT Tier-1 with explicit promotion trigger (non-TS nexus consumer emerges). Latency is a feature, not a defect: it preserves optionality without paying SoT-split tax now.
obstructions.step_4_sanity_check: C-default + B-latent form a valid pair: C is the no-regret action today, B is the prepared response to a witnessed trigger. Drill-round N+1 can promote B by citing the trigger event.
obstructions.step_5_cumulative_learning: ROI questions for cross-runtime ports should default to 'do nothing + prepare codegen' until a witnessed cross-runtime consumer exists. Pre-emptive ports under speculative demand routinely produce SoT-split debt that costs more than the port itself.

obstructions.step_1_identification: typebox schema graph (model-registry.ts:36-178) is recursive, generic, and runtime-validated — the .hexa indented key/value DSL is flat, schema-less, and parse-to-string. Re-expressing typebox in .hexa loses Compile() runtime check and TLocalizedValidationError plumbing.
obstructions.step_2_documentation: OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED: A and E port options carry hidden cost not visible in raw LOC count — loss of typebox validation guarantees.
obstructions.step_3_structural_reframe: Treat typebox schemas as the SoT for any cross-runtime artifact. Codegen target should consume the typebox graph (typebox can emit JSONSchema), not be written by hand. This pushes A/E further into negative-ROI territory and reinforces B-as-codegen.
obstructions.step_4_sanity_check: B as conceived (codegen TS→.hexa) can be implemented as: typebox graph → JSONSchema export → script flattens to .hexa key/value lines. Single source-of-truth preserved at typebox; .hexa is downstream.
obstructions.step_5_cumulative_learning: Schema-rich TS code with runtime validators should never be hand-ported to schema-less DSLs. Codegen-from-validator is the only port shape that preserves the validation contract.
